hypothesis: If tensor completion surrogate models (CPD-S and NeAT) achieve higher R^2 scores than baseline Gaussian Process, XGBoost, and MLP models when predicting Young's modulus E and mass-normalized modulus Ẽ from lattice design parameters under both uniform random and biased sampling schemes, then tensor-structured modeling provides superior predictive accuracy for lattice-property surrogacy due to better exploitation of multi-way parameter dependencies.
